message:
fix(mcp observability-sync): wrap DbConn in Option so Drop routes through explicit close_sync()

`ObservabilitySyncDb` owned a `DbConn` by value. When the struct went
out of scope, the default drop behaviour of `DbConn` logged the
`FrankenConnection` drop_close warn every time an observability sync
cycle ended (hundreds of times per day in production).

Switch the field to `Option<DbConn>`. The `.live()` and stdin/fallback
constructors wrap the connection in `Some(conn)`; the method bodies
unwrap via `as_ref()` / `as_mut()` when running queries; and a future
commit will implement a custom `Drop` that does `if let Some(conn) =
self.conn.take() { conn.close_sync(...) }` so the close fires before
the inner `FrankenConnection` drops.

This commit is the field-shape + constructor rewrite only; the Drop
impl that actually invokes close_sync and the method-body accessor
updates land in the follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
